REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Stacey Hess appeared via zoom for Jennifer Rezentes (petitioner).
Thomas Palmer appeared via zoom for Sharon Stankalis.

Ms. Hess stated that there were errors in the prior tax filings calculations for the distribution and that they will need to adjust and modify their documents prior to getting the final approving settlement. The Petition to Approve Second Status Report on the Estate Administration is ordered off calendar. Status on filing of the approving settlement is set for September 22, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition To Pay The Fee Of The Administrators; Pay The Ordinary Attorneys' Fees And Costs; Distribute The Balance Of The Estate To The Heirs Of The Decedent And Discharge The Petitioner
Off Calendar Aug-14-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Coryell appeared by zoom for Jennifer Rezentes.
Thomas Palmer appeared by zoom for Sharon Stankalis (former administrator).

The court stated that events have overtaken this petition, and that its thought is to take the petition off calendar and to move forward with the newly appointed administrator. The court also noted that a status hearing date of December 18, 2024, has been set for closing out administration of the estate as opposed to the usual one year that is allowed for estate administration. Mr. Coryell stated that he thinks they can make it happen and that he believes all they need to do is to finalize the settlement agreement. The Fourth Amended Petition to Approve the Settlement Agreement, etc. is off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Magdaleno. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-22-2022 Continued To Aug-29-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-20-2022 Continued To Aug-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Coryell, via Zoom, for Jennifer Rezentes, Petitioner; Thomas Palmer, via Zoom, for and with Sharon Stankalis, Personal Representative; Stephanie Dobbs, via Zoom, in propria persona, daughter of Sharon Stankalis.

The court acknowledges Mr. Coryell's objection which is pending in the court's e-filing queue. Mr. Palmer offers an update regarding the status of the $80k and advises that $40k of it has been returned to the estate and that his client is working on restoring the balance. Ms. Dobbs reports that her father, Raymond Elliot, passed-away over the weekend and offers testimony regarding attempts to provide an accounting and offers testimony regarding some of the expenses that her father paid for which they now realize were made using funds obtained without court approval. The court mentions the other issues with this matter specifically, the large amount that is currently deposited in a single bank account at Redwood Credit Union which is not properly insured. The court advises that the money must be distributed amongst several financial institutions so that the accounts do not exceed the limit insured by the FDIC. The court and the parties discuss the possible resolution of this matter with Mr. Palmer advising that there have been issues which have caused the delay in finalizing the settlement of this matter. Mr. Coryell reports that this matter has been delayed much too long and points to the death of two of the three heirs and that this matter could have been closed some time ago and of the failure to make progress toward finalizing a settlement agreement. Ms. Dobbs advises that her mother is motivated to settle and close this matter.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-16-2022 Continued To Jun-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Coryell, via Zoom, for Jennifer Rezentes, Petitioner; Thomas Palmer, via Zoom, for Sharon Stankalis and Raymond Elliot, Administrators.

The court offers its sympathies regarding the recent death of Kenneth but advises of the court's duty to ensure that estates are properly administered. The court confirms that the Personal Representatives have not made any distributions of the proceeds from the sale of the SF property. The court advises counsel that it will be continuing this matter to allow the parties to formally file the following documents:

1) The closing escrow statement for the sale of the estate's San Francisco real property.

2) A current statement for the estate bank account.

The court advises counsel to file the required documents one week prior to the next calling of this matter.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Jun-20-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Coryell appeared by zoom for Jennifer Rezentes.
Thomas Palmer appeared by zoom for Sharon Stankalis and Raymond Elliott (administrators).

Mr. Palmer stated that they did the appraisal of the property and that they have an agreement for his client to purchase the property. Mr. Coryell stated that they reached an agreement, expressed concern about having this wrapped as soon as possible as one of the administrators is having health issues, spoke of the funds being helpful to provide for care and stated that his client is willing to waive an accounting. The court applauded the parties for reaching an agreement and reminded them that they should not be making any distributions without court approval. The Report on Status of Administration, etc. is under submission with the court's intent to grant the petition. Status on close of estate is set for May 16, 2022 at 9;00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Nov-08-2021 Continued To Feb-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Palmer, via ZOOM, for Sharon Stankalis and Raymond Elliot, Co-Administrators; Bryan Coryell for Jennifer Rezentes; Petitioner.

The court queries counsel regarding the Petaluma property buy-out. Mr. Palmer reports that he is waiting for the appraisal on the property has been rescheduled which in turn will determine the counteroffer. Mr. Coryell reports that there has been a delay in obtaining an appraisal. Mr. Coryell states that the Administrator has been living on the property without paying rent and that this matter has been dragging on causing his clients to have to wait for distribution. The court states whether counsel believes there can be a distribution in kind and if waivers of accounting are expected. Mr. Coryell states that he believes that there are no issues with the accounting and that the only issue is the sale of the properties. The court advises counsel that it will not order an accounting at this time and asks if there has been any discussion regarding possibly mediating. Mr. Coryell advises that he does not believe mediation would help but that he will attempt to gather as much information as he can to provide to the Administrator in attempt to resolve this matter.

The Probate Accounting Petition is continued to Feb-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Continued To Jul-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Coryell appeared by telephone for Jennifer Rezentes (Petitioner).
Roxanna Hunt for Sharon Stankalis (Daughter of Decedent).

This was a pre-granted matter. Ms. Hunt stated that she just met with the Client yesterday for the first time, and requested a 45-day continuance to file a formal objection and for Ms. Stankalis to file her own petition to be appointed. Mr. Coryell stated that Ms. Stankalis has had over a decade to do something, that she has been living and using the property for that amount of time, and that she has done nothing. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to July 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
